asi01_agent_goal_hijack.description: Attacker manipulates agent objectives or decision path via malicious input content

direct_prompt_injection.title: Direct Prompt Injection
direct_prompt_injection.description: User-controlled input flows unsanitized into agent system prompt or LLM call
detection_hints: f-string with user input in prompt
format() on prompt string
.invoke(user_input)
.run(user_input)
direct_prompt_injection.remediation: Separate user input from system instructions using distinct message roles. Validate and sanitize all external input before passing to the LLM.

indirect_prompt_injection.title: Indirect Prompt Injection via Tool Output
indirect_prompt_injection.description: Agent processes external tool output or retrieved data containing injected instructions
detection_hints: web search tool result fed to LLM
file read tool output not sanitized
email content passed to agent context
external API response injected into prompt
indirect_prompt_injection.remediation: Treat all tool outputs and retrieved content as untrusted data. Filter tool results for injected instructions before feeding back to the agent context.

goal_manipulation_via_rag.title: Goal Manipulation via RAG or Retrieved Content
goal_manipulation_via_rag.description: Agent retrieves poisoned documents or vector store entries that redirect its objectives
detection_hints: RAG retrieval without content validation
vector store results fed directly to agent
unverified document sources in agent context
goal_manipulation_via_rag.remediation: Validate RAG data sources for integrity. Use multiple independent sources for cross-verification. Lock agent system prompts so goal priorities are explicit and auditable.

asi02_tool_misuse.description: Agent uses legitimate tools in unsafe ways due to over-privilege, bad input, or poisoned descriptors

excessive_tool_permissions.title: Excessive Tool Permissions
excessive_tool_permissions.description: Agent granted tools far beyond what its stated goal requires — violates least-agency principle
detection_hints: shell tool registered on narrow-purpose agent
broad filesystem or DB access for read-only task
admin-level tools granted to customer-facing agent
excessive_tool_permissions.remediation: Apply least-privilege principle: grant only tools the agent's stated purpose requires. Classify tools by risk level and restrict high-impact tools (send, delete, execute) to trusted contexts.

unvalidated_tool_input.title: Unvalidated Tool Input Parameters
unvalidated_tool_input.description: Tool parameters flow from LLM output to function calls without type, range, or semantic validation
detection_hints: no input validation on tool arguments
direct use of LLM-generated args in sensitive functions
no schema enforcement on tool parameters
unvalidated_tool_input.remediation: Validate all tool input parameters for type, range, and format before execution. Use allowlists for permitted values. Block chained command patterns.

poisoned_tool_descriptor.title: Poisoned or Tampered Tool Descriptor
poisoned_tool_descriptor.description: Tool definition or MCP server descriptor has been modified to redirect agent actions
detection_hints: tool definition loaded from external or unverified source
MCP server URL not pinned or verified
dynamic tool registration without manifest signing
poisoned_tool_descriptor.remediation: Verify tool definitions against a trusted registry. Pin tool versions and validate checksums. Monitor for changes to tool schemas at runtime.

path_traversal.title: Path Traversal in File Tools
path_traversal.description: File read/write tools accept paths without sanitization, enabling directory traversal
detection_hints: FileReadTool or FileWriteTool with no path restriction
open(path) with path derived from LLM output
no path.resolve() or os.path.abspath() sanitization
path_traversal.remediation: Restrict file paths to a sandbox directory using Path.resolve() and startswith() validation. Reject paths containing '..' or absolute paths outside the allowed root.

asi03_identity_privilege_abuse.description: Agents inherit, reuse, or escalate identities and credentials beyond their functional scope

hardcoded_credentials.title: Hardcoded API Keys or Credentials
hardcoded_credentials.description: API keys, tokens, or passwords hardcoded in agent source or config files
detection_hints: api_key= literal string assignment
OPENAI_API_KEY = '...' hardcoded
sk- prefix in source file
password or secret literal in config
hardcoded_credentials.remediation: Load credentials from environment variables or a secrets manager (e.g., AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault). Never store API keys, tokens, or passwords in source code.

missing_auth_on_endpoint.title: Missing Authentication on Agent Endpoint
missing_auth_on_endpoint.description: Agent server or API endpoint exposed without authentication or TLS
detection_hints: app.run() with no auth middleware
uvicorn.run(host='0.0.0.0') with no token check
FastAPI/Flask routes with no authentication decorator
HTTP server binding to 0.0.0.0
missing_auth_on_endpoint.remediation: Enable TLS on all agent endpoints. Implement authentication (OAuth 2.0, API key verification) and restrict binding to specific network interfaces.

over_privileged_agent.title: Over-Privileged Agent Role or Identity
over_privileged_agent.description: Agent role, backstory, or system prompt grants capabilities beyond its functional need
detection_hints: allow_delegation=True with no scope restriction
admin or superuser role in backstory
full filesystem access declared in system prompt
over_privileged_agent.remediation: Restrict the agent's system prompt and role to only necessary capabilities. Remove broad permissions ('full access', 'admin') from agent instructions.

confused_deputy.title: Confused Deputy Attack
confused_deputy.description: Agent manipulated into using its legitimate credentials to act on behalf of an attacker
detection_hints: agent uses stored OAuth/API credentials without scope check
no action scope restriction on credential-bearing tools
broad API tool access with no per-request authorization
confused_deputy.remediation: Validate the identity and authorization of all callers before executing actions on their behalf. Use scoped credentials that cannot be reused across contexts.

inherited_session_abuse.title: Inherited Session or Token Abuse
inherited_session_abuse.description: Agent inherits user session, OAuth token, or SSH key with scope broader than needed
detection_hints: agent stores SSH or OAuth tokens in memory
credentials passed across agent boundaries without downscoping
user session token shared between multiple agents
inherited_session_abuse.remediation: Issue agent-specific credentials with minimal scope instead of inheriting user sessions. Use short-lived tokens and rotate credentials regularly.

asi04_supply_chain.description: Compromised runtime components — tools, plugins, MCP servers, deps — alter agent behavior

unpinned_dependencies.title: Unpinned or Overly Broad Dependency Versions
unpinned_dependencies.description: requirements.txt uses unpinned or wildcard versions enabling supply chain substitution
detection_hints: >= version constraint on AI framework package
~= or * version in requirements
no version pin on crewai, langchain, openai, anthropic
unpinned_dependencies.remediation: Pin all dependency versions with == in requirements files. Use a lock file to ensure reproducible builds. Run dependency scanning in CI.

known_vulnerable_dependency.title: Known Vulnerable Dependency (CVE)
known_vulnerable_dependency.description: A package in the dependency tree has a known CVE in the OSV/NVD database
detection_hints: pip-audit findings
OSV.dev advisory matches
known_vulnerable_dependency.remediation: Upgrade the affected package to the fixed version. If no fix exists, evaluate alternatives or apply compensating controls.

untrusted_external_tool.title: Untrusted or Unverified External Tool/Plugin
untrusted_external_tool.description: Agent dynamically loads tools, MCP servers, or plugins without signature or registry verification
detection_hints: MCP server URL loaded from config without pinning
dynamic tool import without hash verification
third-party plugin loaded without signed manifest
untrusted_external_tool.remediation: Source tools and MCP servers from trusted registries only. Verify signatures and scan code/dependencies before deployment. Proxy agent requests through a gateway.

asi05_unexpected_code_execution.description: Agent generates or runs code, commands, or scripts without safe sandboxing or review

arbitrary_code_execution.title: Arbitrary Code Execution via Tool or eval()
arbitrary_code_execution.description: Agent executes shell commands or eval() with parameters derived from LLM output or user input
detection_hints: eval(x) where x comes from LLM or user
exec(script_content)
subprocess.run(shell=True)
os.system(cmd)
CodeInterpreterTool with no sandbox
arbitrary_code_execution.remediation: Replace eval()/exec() with safe alternatives (ast.literal_eval(), operator-based parsers). Use subprocess with argument lists, never shell=True. Sandbox code execution in containers.

unsafe_code_generation.title: Generated Code Executed Without Review
unsafe_code_generation.description: Agent-generated code or patches applied directly without human preview or sandboxed testing
detection_hints: code generation tool with auto-apply flag
generated patches run without review step
no human_input=True before code execution task
unsafe_code_generation.remediation: Review all agent-generated code before execution. Run generated code in a sandboxed environment with restricted filesystem and network access.

unsafe_deserialization.title: Unsafe Deserialization of Agent State
unsafe_deserialization.description: Agent deserializes untrusted data (pickle, YAML load, JSON with class hooks) enabling code execution
detection_hints: pickle.loads() on external data
yaml.load() without SafeLoader
json deserialization with object_hook
unsafe_deserialization.remediation: Replace pickle.loads() with json.loads(). Use yaml.safe_load() instead of yaml.load(). Never deserialize data from untrusted sources.

asi06_memory_context_poisoning.description: Persistent memory, RAG stores, or context summaries poisoned to alter future agent behavior

memory_poisoning.title: Agent Memory Poisoning
memory_poisoning.description: Persistent agent memory stores unsanitized content that poisons future reasoning sessions
detection_hints: memory=True with no content filter
store_memory() called on unvalidated output
append to memory without sanitization
memory_poisoning.remediation: Scan all memory writes for malicious content before commit. Track provenance of memory entries. Use expiry and down-weighting for unverified memories.

persistent_memory_no_sanitization.title: Persistent Memory Without Content Filtering
persistent_memory_no_sanitization.description: Agent stores conversation history or tool outputs persistently with no PII or injection filtering
detection_hints: ChromaDB or vector store write without filter
memory=True on CrewAI agent or crew
no content filter before vector store ingestion
persistent_memory_no_sanitization.remediation: Filter PII, credentials, and injection patterns from content before storing in memory. Implement content classification and redaction rules.

cross_session_contamination.title: Cross-Session or Cross-Tenant Memory Contamination
cross_session_contamination.description: Shared memory or state between agent sessions exposes data from one user or tenant to another
detection_hints: global memory object shared across requests
vector DB without tenant/user isolation
Crew memory=True with shared vector store
cross_session_contamination.remediation: Use per-session or per-tenant memory with UUID-based isolation. Clear shared state between sessions. Enforce memory namespace boundaries.

rag_poisoning.title: RAG Database Poisoning
rag_poisoning.description: Attacker injects adversarial content into RAG knowledge base to manipulate agent responses
detection_hints: RAG ingestion without source validation
unverified external documents added to vector store
no provenance tracking on RAG entries
rag_poisoning.remediation: Validate and authenticate all documents before indexing into RAG. Implement content integrity checks. Monitor for anomalous insertions into vector stores.

asi07_insecure_interagent_comms.description: Agent-to-agent messages lack authentication, encryption, or semantic validation

unauthenticated_agent_communication.title: Unauthenticated Agent-to-Agent Communication
unauthenticated_agent_communication.description: Messages between agents carry no cryptographic identity proof — any party can spoof an agent
detection_hints: agent communication with no sender authentication
GroupChat with no speaker verification
agent messages trusted implicitly by role name
unauthenticated_agent_communication.remediation: Require mutual authentication (mTLS, signed tokens) for all agent-to-agent communication. Verify sender identity before processing messages.

unencrypted_agent_channel.title: Unencrypted Agent Communication Channel
unencrypted_agent_channel.description: Inter-agent communication sent over plain HTTP or unencrypted channels — susceptible to interception
detection_hints: http:// URL used for agent endpoint communication
no TLS configured on agent server
plain socket communication between agents
unencrypted_agent_channel.remediation: Use TLS/HTTPS for all inter-agent communication. Never transmit agent messages over plain HTTP. Enable certificate verification.

unvalidated_agent_message.title: Unvalidated Inter-Agent Message Content
unvalidated_agent_message.description: Agent blindly trusts and acts on messages from other agents without semantic or schema validation
detection_hints: agent trusts all inputs from upstream agent without validation
no message schema enforcement between agents
inter-agent results used directly without sanity check
unvalidated_agent_message.remediation: Validate all incoming agent messages against an expected schema. Reject messages with unexpected fields, types, or instruction patterns.

asi08_cascading_failures.description: Errors in one agent propagate across multi-agent pipelines with compounding destructive impact

unbounded_agent_loop.title: Unbounded Agent Execution Loop
unbounded_agent_loop.description: Agent loop has no max iteration count or timeout — enables resource exhaustion or runaway execution
detection_hints: max_iter not set on agent or crew
no timeout on agent execution
while True in agent execution loop
unbounded_agent_loop.remediation: Set max_iterations, max_turns, or recursion_limit on all agent loops. Add timeout mechanisms. Implement circuit breakers to halt runaway execution.

missing_circuit_breaker.title: Missing Circuit Breaker Between Agents
missing_circuit_breaker.description: No isolation or failure boundary between agents — a failure in one propagates unchecked to all downstream
detection_hints: no error handling between agent task calls
agent chain with no retry or fallback logic
downstream agent receives unvalidated upstream output
missing_circuit_breaker.remediation: Add failure isolation boundaries between agents. Implement circuit breakers that trip when error rates exceed thresholds. Limit blast radius of individual agent failures.

missing_blast_radius_limit.title: No Blast Radius or Rate Limit on Agent Actions
missing_blast_radius_limit.description: Agent can invoke unlimited downstream actions or API calls without throttling or scope caps
detection_hints: no rate limiting on tool invocations
agent spawns sub-agents with no concurrency limit
recursive agent delegation with no depth cap
missing_blast_radius_limit.remediation: Set rate limits and quotas on agent API calls and downstream actions. Cap the number of operations per execution cycle.

asi09_human_agent_trust_exploitation.description: Users manipulated into harmful decisions by trusting persuasive or authoritative agent outputs

no_human_in_the_loop.title: No Human-in-the-Loop for Sensitive Actions
no_human_in_the_loop.description: Agent executes sensitive operations (file write, code exec, payments) without mandatory human approval
detection_hints: human_input_mode=NEVER on sensitive agent
no human_input=True on high-impact tasks
auto-approve flag on action-taking agent
no_human_in_the_loop.remediation: Require human approval for sensitive operations (file writes, deletions, payments, code execution). Use approval hooks or human_input_mode settings.

sensitive_data_in_output.title: Sensitive Data Logged or Output Without Redaction
sensitive_data_in_output.description: Agent outputs or logs expose PII, credentials, or confidential data without filtering
detection_hints: print(result) with full LLM response
verbose=True on agent handling sensitive context
logging full tool output without PII scrubbing
sensitive_data_in_output.remediation: Implement output filtering to redact PII, credentials, and internal data before returning results. Use DLP rules and pattern-based scrubbing.

missing_action_confirmation.title: Missing Confirmation Gate for High-Risk Actions
missing_action_confirmation.description: High-impact actions (delete, send, pay, deploy) executed without explicit user confirmation step
detection_hints: delete or drop operations in agent tool with no confirm step
financial transaction tool without approval gate
deploy or publish action without human sign-off
missing_action_confirmation.remediation: Add explicit confirmation gates before destructive / high-impact operations (delete, drop, send, deploy). Require user input before proceeding with high-impact actions.

persuasive_agent_language.title: Persuasive or Manipulative Agent Output Patterns
persuasive_agent_language.description: Agent system prompt or backstory instructs it to use persuasive language that may manipulate users into unsafe decisions
detection_hints: backstory instructs agent to be persuasive
goal includes convincing or influencing users
system prompt uses emotional or urgency language
persuasive_agent_language.remediation: Audit agent system prompts for persuasive or manipulative language patterns. Ensure agent output is factual and neutral, not designed to pressure user decisions.

asi10_rogue_agents.description: Compromised or misaligned agents act outside their intended scope without external triggering

missing_kill_switch.title: No Kill Switch or Emergency Stop Mechanism
missing_kill_switch.description: Agent system has no mechanism to immediately halt or terminate agent execution
detection_hints: no max_iter or timeout on agent
no signal handler or shutdown mechanism
no graceful termination path in agent code
missing_kill_switch.remediation: Implement an emergency stop mechanism to immediately halt agent execution across all endpoints. Maintain a centralized policy to disable agents organization-wide.

missing_behavioral_guardrails.title: No Behavioral Constraints or Guardrails
missing_behavioral_guardrails.description: Agent has no explicit operational constraints — can take any action its tools allow without scope limits
detection_hints: agent goal is open-ended with no explicit action boundaries
no denylist or allowlist on tool usage
system prompt sets no scope restrictions
missing_behavioral_guardrails.remediation: Define explicit operational constraints in the agent's configuration. Add input and output guardrails. Enforce output schema validation.

unchecked_agent_delegation.title: Unchecked Agent Delegation
unchecked_agent_delegation.description: Agent can delegate tasks to sub-agents with no scope, identity, or depth constraints
detection_hints: allow_delegation=True with no whitelist
GroupChat with no speaker selection policy
recursive agent spawning with no depth limit
unchecked_agent_delegation.remediation: Add callbacks before/after agent calls or input guardrails on delegating agents. Validate delegated tasks against a scope allowlist. Limit delegation depth.

missing_agent_monitoring.title: No Agent Behavioral Monitoring or Observability
missing_agent_monitoring.description: Agent actions, tool calls, and decisions are not logged or monitored — rogue behavior is undetectable
detection_hints: no logging of tool invocations
no audit trail for agent decisions
verbose=False with no alternative observability
missing_agent_monitoring.remediation: Enable structured logging of all tool invocations, decisions, and outputs. Set verbose=True or integrate with observability platforms. Monitor for behavioral drift.
